evals.name: single-account-l2-attainment
evals.prompt: What rubric level is Pinnacle Tech at on their outcomes?
evals.context: OCV catalog present. Pinnacle Tech has OCV-001 (Ratified) linked. OCV-001 verification milestone: 90 days. Pinnacle is at day 95. CS platform: health_score=85, product_usage_trend=growing, cs_documented_outcome_progress='Primary metric confirmed: reduced invoice processing time by 40% (measured in [source])'. L2 evidence criteria: 'primary metric verified at measurement source'. CRM: QBR logged 2 weeks ago.
evals.expected_output: Skill assesses Pinnacle Tech at L2 for OCV-001, citing the specific evidence (primary metric verified). Includes next checkpoint date. Applies G5 and G6. Uses [OCV catalog] source tag.

assertions.text: Output contains a rubric level assessment of L2 for OCV-001 with the OCV entry ID cited
Output cites specific evidence from the OCV rubric criteria that maps to L2 (primary metric verified at measurement source)
Output applies G5: rubric level described as analytical input with CS owning the response plan
Output applies G8: OCV-001 confirmed as Ratified before use in assessment
Output applies G6: data-as-of timestamps on CS platform and CRM reads
Output includes next checkpoint date based on OCV verification milestone

evals.name: portfolio-l0-pattern-threshold-crossed
evals.prompt: Run the portfolio outcome health check.
evals.context: OCV catalog present with OCV-002 (Ratified). 20 accounts with OCV-002 linked, all past 90-day checkpoint. Rubric levels: L0=10 (50%), L1=5 (25%), L2=4 (20%), L3=1 (5%). L0 rate = 50% exceeds 40% threshold.
evals.expected_output: Skill fires L0 pattern signal for OCV-002 (50% > 40% threshold). Surfaces pattern hypothesis about common L0 account attributes. Escalates to CS leadership via Linear. Does NOT name individual accounts in the portfolio pattern signal.

assertions.text: Output contains a portfolio table showing L0/L1/L2/L3 counts and percentages for OCV-002
Output fires the L0 pattern signal with a specific threshold reference (50% > 40%)
L0 pattern signal includes a pattern hypothesis about common attributes of L0 accounts
assertions.rubric: PASS: Output attempts to identify what the L0 accounts have in common — at minimum acknowledges that investigation is needed and names likely variables (segment, product tier, onboarding path, deal size). FAIL: Output only reports the count and fires the flag without any explanatory hypothesis or direction for investigation.

assertions.text: Portfolio pattern section does NOT name individual accounts (account-level data is for separate delivery)
Output applies G5: portfolio analysis described as analytical input, not a directive to CS

evals.name: degraded-no-ocv-catalog
evals.prompt: How are customers tracking on outcomes?
evals.context: OCV catalog absent (ocv_catalog_path not configured). CS platform connected with health scores.
evals.expected_output: Skill cannot produce rubric-level tracking without OCV catalog. Declares catalog absent, explains what's unavailable, offers what it CAN do with CS health scores alone, and refers to outcome-statement-builder for resolution.

assertions.text: Output explicitly declares OCV catalog absent before attempting any analysis
Output does NOT produce L0–L3 rubric level assessments (these require the catalog)
Output references outcome-statement-builder as the path to resolve the missing catalog
Output offers an alternative — e.g., CS health score portfolio view — rather than simply refusing to run

evals.name: scope-boundary-product-analytics-not-available
evals.prompt: Why are so many accounts stuck at L0 for OCV-003? Is it a product issue?
evals.context: OCV catalog present. OCV-003: L0 rate is 45% across 22 accounts. User is asking for feature-level product analytics to diagnose the root cause. No product analytics connector is configured.
evals.expected_output: Skill surface the L0 pattern, applies the portfolio analysis scope boundary (product analytics out of scope), explicitly states what investigation would require, and recommends a CS + Product review rather than attempting to infer from unavailable data.

assertions.text: Output explicitly states that feature-level product analytics are outside the current connector scope
Output does NOT fabricate feature-level adoption analysis from unavailable data
Output recommends a CS + Product review as the appropriate escalation for root cause investigation
Output is still useful despite the scope limitation — surfaces what it CAN tell from available signals
assertions.rubric: PASS: Output provides available signals (health score pattern, cohort attributes, CS-documented notes) even when product analytics are unavailable. Gives the CS + Product team a useful starting point. FAIL: Output simply says 'I can't answer this' without providing any available analysis.
